channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kamaraj's Governance Legacy: Lessons in Leadership and Development
item.description: The article explores the enduring impact of K. Kamaraj, former Chief Minister of Madras State, on modern Tamil Nadu governance. It highlights his people-centric approach, administrative acumen, and em...

item.title: Philippines and China maintain South China Sea code of conduct stance
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing diplomatic efforts between the Philippines and China regarding the South China Sea code of conduct. Despite tensions, both nations continue to emphasize the importanc...

item.title: Upcoming Football Competitions After the 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has concluded, but fans can still enjoy a packed schedule of club competitions and leagues across Europe, the US, and Australia. The A-League Men's competition in Australia wil...

item.title: Ibrahim Ambwere's Journey from Sweeper to Billionaire and His Teacher Payment Offer
item.description: The article details the life of Ibrahim Ambwere, a self-made billionaire from Western Kenya, and his daughter Melody Sinzore, a prominent radio presenter. Ambwere, who started as a sweeper and carpent...

item.title: Johannesburg and National Treasury Finalise Financial Stabilisation Measures for City
item.description: The City of Johannesburg and National Treasury have reached an agreement on immediate financial stabilisation measures to address the metro's severe liquidity crisis. With less than two weeks of cash ...

item.title: UK Inflation Expected to Drop in June Amid Oil Price Falls and Energy Cost Concerns
item.description: Official UK inflation data for June is expected to show a temporary decline, driven by falling petrol and diesel prices following an interim ceasefire between the US and Iran. Economists anticipate th...

item.title: NY mayor says ICC arrest warrant for Netanyahu cannot be enforced locally, urges federal action
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has stated that his administration lacks the legal authority to enforce the International Criminal Court's (ICC) arrest warrant against Israeli Prime Minister Benjam...

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Brings Coastal Flooding and Strong Winds to Gulf Coast
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is moving slowly along the Gulf Coast, posing threats of coastal flooding, gusty winds, and heavy rainfall from Florida to Louisiana. As of Tuesday evening, Bertha's maximum sust...

item.title: DOTr Provides Free Commuter Rides During Transport Strike
item.description: The Department of Transportation (DOTr) and other government agencies have launched a free ride initiative to assist commuters affected by the ongoing transport strike. This measure, aligned with Pres...

item.title: Lebanon's president seeks US backing for Israeli withdrawal and military support amid regional tensions
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun has met with US President Donald Trump at the White House to seek political and military support for efforts to secure an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon and di...

item.title: Indian Sailors Killed in US Airstrike on Drifting Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman
item.description: The oil tanker Settebello, carrying Iranian crude oil, was struck by a US airstrike in the Gulf of Oman while drifting with its engine off. Three Indian sailors—chief engineer Suresh Patnala, mechanic...

item.title: Pathologist confirms manual strangling as cause of death for woman in Kitengela
item.description: A pathologist at Kitengela Sub-County Hospital confirmed that manual strangling caused the death of a woman allegedly killed by her ex-boyfriend. The autopsy revealed bruising on the neck indicating s...

item.title: Yohanna Kusau: Champion of Primary Healthcare in Nigeria
item.description: Yohanna Kusau, a primary healthcare worker in Nigeria, has become a symbol of dedication and leadership in the fight to improve community health. His story highlights how small, consistent actions can...

item.title: Gen Z Movements and Youth-led Protests in South Asia: A Comparative Analysis
item.description: The article explores the four key factors driving youth-led protests that ousted leaders in Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, and Nepal, while questioning India's unique position. Educated unemployment at scale ...

item.title: FIFA Considers Hosting World Cup in the US Again by 2038
item.description: The article discusses FIFA's growing interest in hosting the FIFA World Cup in the United States as early as 2038, driven by significant revenue from ticket sales and hospitality. Donald Trump has pub...

item.title: Sharon Stone's Journey to Drug-Free Living After Stroke
item.description: Hollywood icon Sharon Stone has shared her challenging decision to discontinue her stroke medication and cannabis use, aiming for a completely substance-free lifestyle. Despite strict medical warnings...

item.title: U.S. Announces New Tariffs to Replace Expiring Global Levy
item.description: The United States is preparing to implement new tariffs targeting over 60 trading partners, replacing the temporary 10% global levy imposed by President Donald Trump. These new tariffs, ranging betwee...

item.title: Govender brothers face murder trial after witness alleges prior threats
item.description: The trial of Ferrel and Darren Govender, accused of murdering Shailen Singh in December 2024, commenced at the Durban High Court. A confidential witness testified that Ferrel Govender threatened him i...

item.title: Trump Announces Plan to Impose Tariffs on Generic Drugs by 2028
item.description: President Donald Trump announced during a press briefing that the U.S. plans to impose tariffs on generic drugs by 2028, citing the need to protect domestic pharmaceutical companies and reduce relianc...

item.title: Celebrity divorces and personal struggles dominate headlines
item.description: The article compiles a series of celebrity news stories highlighting dramatic life events. It begins with Davina McCall's remarriage to hairdresser Michael Douglas, showcasing their lavish Kent weddin...
